channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Furniture broken recycling blue bin. Dumped, 30th April
item.description:
Dressing table mattress blue broken recycling bin dumped next to our communal recycling bins presumed not reported as they have been there since Monday

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress and other large items, 30th April
item.description:
Mattress and other bulky items left in Kingsgate Estate bin chamber on De Beauvoir Road

item.title: Dumped sofa and cushions, 30th April
item.description:
Sofa seen thrown over wall from Brookside court last night

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 148, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London

item.title: Debris from crash at kerbside, 30th April
item.description:
After a crash last week, the car has been removed and debris has been left. Some small pieces or plastic/glass as well in roadside.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wickham Street, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bainbridge street, 30th April
item.description:
Second bollard knocked out of the ground by large trucks using the road and unable to pass illegal parking. site won't allow me to upload photos but there is a considerable hole in the ground and the bollard has been hit a few times and is now six ft or more from its place and on public footpath. If these bollards are here for public safety then the parking on double yellows and forcing large vehicles to mount the curb should be stopped also. Can this be passed to someone to increase enforcement or change this to a red route/ delivery drop off only?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 269, Tottenham Court Road, Covent Garden, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 30th April
item.description:
There is a dead fox on the corner of St Augustine’s and Kingswood, it is on the corner of the property of the abandoned demolished Old Leather Pub. This needs to be removed before it starts decomposing and creates a public health problem. As the property is abandoned it is unlikely the owner of the property is aware of the dead fox.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kingswood Avenue, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Coronation court, 30th April
item.description:
Rubbish has not been collected for two weeks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kenilworth Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lamp post, fence and wall have graffiti on, 30th April
item.description:
What3words ///nearly.wake.outer Spray tags on silver lamp post, wooden fence and brick wall at the top of the park. Between Tannsfield Rd and Cator Rd entrances.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Allendale Close, Perry Vale, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road Surface Condition potentially cause damage to Properties, 30th April
item.description:
Over the past month, we have experienced increasing levels of vibration and noise at our property when buses and heavy goods vehicles pass over a number of previously reported potholes on Petts Wood Road (between 120 and 168 of the road). We have spoken with several neighbours on this section of the road, who have reported similar issues. The vibrations are causing windows to rattle, timber fixtures to creak, and even ceramic plant pots to shift on the windowsills. We are concerned that, if this continues, it may result in lasting damage to our properties and possessions. We would therefore urge the Council to prioritise resurfacing this section of the road as soon as possible.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 139, Petts Wood Road, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No space for regular bicycles, 30th April
item.description:
Every morning I come to the same spot and there is literally nowhere to lock my bike I have to start moving Lime bikes around to free some space

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Phipp Street, Shoreditch, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped sofa and cupboard, 30th April
item.description:
Witnessed two men dumping sofa and cupboard on pavement at 12.30am - photographed - and running off when challenged. Ran into Brookside Court and then seen dumping further rubbish on Versailles.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 148, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent Road Safety Concern – Warepoint Drive (Bus Stop BP2573, Blue Bird Way, North Greenwich), 30th April
item.description:
I am writing to report a serious road safety issue on Warepoint Drive, near the bus stop at Blue Bird Way (BP2573) in North Greenwich. There is a curve immediately after the bus stop where visibility is already limited. On this stretch, there are no yellow lines, which allows vehicles to park freely. Cars are often parked very close to the bend and directly in front of residential driveways. This creates a dangerous situation, especially when buses are stopped and additional vehicles are parked along the same side. Visibility for oncoming traffic is significantly reduced, particularly around the curve. There have been multiple incidents and near misses due to this issue. This is an ongoing hazard that puts both drivers and pedestrians at risk. I kindly request an urgent review of this location and consideration of safety measures such as: * Double yellow lines along the curved section * Parking restrictions near the bus stop and bend * A visibility and traffic risk assessment This issue requires prompt attention before a serious accident occurs. Thank you for your time and consideration.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Warepoint Drive, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Back garden of 14C lady Somerset road, 30th April
item.description:
Tree branches are over hanging and hitting the window. Residents son is allergic to the tree and yesterday paramedics had to be called as his reaction was quite severe so they desperately need the tree cutting back.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Oakford Road, Dartmouth Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
